tikz-formalize-LP-01-0f3fd5ab: Schematic of a convex combination on a 2D plane: two black points x and y are joined by a line segment, with a red point z placed one-third of the way from y to x to illustrate z = lambda*x + (1-lambda)*y for lambda = 2/3.
tikz-formalize-LP-02-e734785f: Two side-by-side shapes contrasting a convex set (a tilted gray ellipse) with a non-convex set (a curvy gray blob with a notch); a chord between two interior dots leaves the second shape, demonstrating non-convexity.
tikz-formalize-LP-03-8355e413: Two-dimensional plot showing a halfspace: the line through (-1.5, 1.5) and (1.5, -1.5) splits the plane on x_1, x_2 axes, with the upper side shaded blue and labelled Halfspace.
tikz-formalize-LP-04-362d2361: Two-dimensional plot showing a polyhedron: a small green four-sided polygon with vertices near (0.5,0.5), (1.5,0.2), (1.2,-0.8), (0.2,-0.5), labelled Polyhedron, on x_1, x_2 axes.
tikz-formalize-LP-05-002a134a: Two-dimensional plot showing a ball: a red disk of radius 1 centered at the origin, labelled Ball, on x_1, x_2 axes -- an example of a convex set defined by sum of x_i^2 <= 1.
tikz-formalize-LP-06-a88769f7: Two-dimensional plot showing a second-order cone: an upward orange triangular wedge with apex at (0,2) and base from (-1,0) to (1,0), illustrating {(x,t) : ||x||^2 <= t^2} on x_1, x_2 axes.
tikz-formalize-LP-07-0093c960: Two-dimensional plot of a blue ball overlapping a red six-sided polytope; the lens-shaped overlap is shaded green. A short segment between two interior points x and y illustrates that the intersection of two convex sets is convex.
tikz-formalize-LP-08-cbc2f304: One-dimensional number line showing c^T z between c^T x and c^T y. Three dots mark min and max of {c^T x, c^T y} and the convex combination c^T z = lambda c^T x + (1-lambda) c^T y in between.
tikz-formalize-LP-09-5aaa45c8: Two-dimensional plot of a quadrilateral polytope with vertices (0,0), (1,2), (3,2), (4,0). A green vertex v and two interior red points x and y each show short arrows v +/- epsilon*d, distinguishing perturbations that stay inside from those that leave the polytope.
tikz-formalize-LP-10-5cbd73ce: Two-dimensional plot of an LP feasible region on a 0-5 grid: shaded blue polygon with extreme points (0,0), (0,1), (2,3), bounded by s_1=0 (red), s_2=0 (teal), s_3=0 (orange) lines; vertices marked with blue dots and red squares.
tikz-Section2-01-d570467c: Two-dimensional plot of the feasible region for max 2x_1+5x_2 with vertices (0,0), (9,0), (6,5), (0,8), bounded by x_1+2x_2<=16 and 5x_1+3x_2<=45 (red); an inset table lists the objective value at each vertex (0, 18, 37, 40).
tikz-Section2-02-9c4fb6c5: Two-dimensional plot of the Furniture Workshop feasible region: a blue pentagon with vertices (0,0), (35,0), (35,10), (30,20), (0,30) bounded by 2x_1+x_2<=80, x_1+3x_2<=90, x_1<=35; dashed level curves 8x_1+6x_2 = 120, 240, 360 sweep upward.
tikz-Section2-03-833384fe: Two-dimensional plot showing alternative optimal solutions: same Furniture Workshop pentagon with dashed level curves of 2x_1+x_2 (z = 20, 40, 60, 80). The z=80 curve overlays the entire edge from (30,20) to (35,10), highlighted as a thick blue segment.
tikz-Section2-04-14a96534: Two-dimensional plot of an infeasible LP: a red triangle (x_1+x_2<=8) and blue triangle (2x_1+x_2<=12) sit lower-left while a vertical line x_1=7 (green) and horizontal x_2=5 (orange) require points upper-right; a No feasible region label fills the gap.
tikz-Section2-05-e6614181: Two-dimensional plot of an unbounded feasible region for min 5x_1+7x_2 extending up and right, bounded by x_1+3x_2>=6 (red), 5x_1+2x_2>=10 (green), x_2<=4 (purple); dashed level curves of 3x_1+2x_2 mark the optimum at lower-left vertex (1.38, 1.54).
tikz-Section2-06-c779dbe3: Two-dimensional plot of an unbounded LP for max x_1+3x_2: blue feasible region extends infinitely up and right with vertices (0,2) and (10/3, 1/3); dashed level curves z = 3, 6, 9, 12, 15 keep intersecting the region, so the objective is unbounded.
tikz-Section2-07-452cfb64: Two-dimensional plot of the same unbounded region under min 3x_1+x_2. Dashed level curves z = 2, 4, 6, 8 sweep from the lower-left; the smallest curve meeting the region passes through (0,2), marked as the finite optimum with z* = 2.
tikz-Section2-08-0233be68: Data table styled as a figure listing three dietary supplement products with cost and milligrams of nutrients M and N -- the input data for the diet LP (minimize cost subject to nutrient requirements).
tikz-Section2-09-b8c094b3: Two-dimensional plot of the LP feasible region for max -5x_1-x_2 with extreme points (0,0), (0,1), (2,3), shaded blue on a 0-5 grid. Lines s_1=0 (red), s_2=0 (teal), s_3=0 (orange) bound the region; vertices marked with blue dots and red squares.
tikz-Section2-10-202fcef4: Two-dimensional plot replotting the same feasible region (extreme points (0,0), (0,1), (2,3)) but with s_1=0 (red) and s_2=0 (teal) drawn as full plotted lines rather than just segments, to discuss extending the constraint lines.
tikz-Section2-11-821cbf21: Two-dimensional plot of the recession cone obtained by setting b=0: a thin blue triangular cone bounded by s_1=0 (red), s_2=0 (teal), s_3=0 (orange) emanating from the origin, with the line x_1+x_2=1 drawn from (0,1) to (1,0) across it.
tikz-Section2-12-821cbf21: Two-dimensional plot of the same recession cone with rays s_1=0, s_2=0, s_3=0 from the origin and the line x_1+x_2=1, used to isolate the line segment of extreme directions inside the cone.
tikz-Section2-13-2556d599: Two-dimensional plot magnifying the segment x_1+x_2=1 inside the recession cone, showing extreme directions (4/5, 1/5) and (2/3, 1/3) as red boxes with blue dots; bounded by s_1=0 (red) and s_3=0 (orange), with redundant s_2=0 removed.
